issues.evidence: `assessment.responses` accessed in ReportService.py line ~51 but Assessment model has no `responses` Column. Defined columns: ['assessment_data', 'client_id', 'created_at', 'id', 'updated_at']
issues.issue: Service ReportService.py accesses Assessment.responses which does not exist on the model — will raise AttributeError at runtime.
issues.fix: Add `responses = Column(String, nullable=True)` to business/models/Assessment.py, OR remove the reference to `assessment.responses` in ReportService.py and use an existing field instead.

issues.evidence: `assessment.score` accessed in ReportService.py line ~52 but Assessment model has no `score` Column. Defined columns: ['assessment_data', 'client_id', 'created_at', 'id', 'updated_at']
issues.issue: Service ReportService.py accesses Assessment.score which does not exist on the model — will raise AttributeError at runtime.
issues.fix: Add `score = Column(String, nullable=True)` to business/models/Assessment.py, OR remove the reference to `assessment.score` in ReportService.py and use an existing field instead.

issues.evidence: Intake specifies PDF output but no PDF generation library found. Checked: reportlab, fpdf, weasyprint, pdfkit, pypdf, xhtml2pdf
issues.issue: Intake requires downloadable PDF reports but no PDF library is present. Download endpoint returns CSV/JSON only.
issues.fix: Add `reportlab` to requirements.txt. In reports.py download endpoint, add a 'pdf' format branch: use reportlab to generate PDF from report_data and return as StreamingResponse with media_type='application/pdf'.

issues.evidence: KPI 'MVP' defined in intake but not referenced anywhere in artifacts
issues.issue: Intake KPI 'MVP' has no implementation in any service or route.
issues.fix: Add `_calculate_mvp()` method to ScoringService.py or ReportService.py and include 'MVP' in the KPI output dict.
